observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Accountant to record the Land in Barangay XI out by the submitted appraised value of all the donated real properties. Reconcile Accounting with related records of the landholdings of the municipality;
b) The Municipal Budget Officer to allocate in the ensuing years’ budget the cost for titling of Land; and
c) All concerned department heads to coordinate with each other and exert extra efforts to secure the documents relative to the purchase made from Mr. Reynaldo Cargado. Work together towards the reconciliation and registration of all landholdings to protect the municipality against undue risk of third party claim.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant reasoned out that he had not effect the corrections due to no available source documents.
The Municipal Budget Officer had allocated the budget in CY 2022.
All concerned department heads to coordinate; The Management commented that this pandemic hindered them to coordinate with each other.
observations.reason: Pandemic hindered them to coordinate with each other.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC, in coordination with the GAD Focal Point, to:
1. spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database, manual or electronic, consisting of sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information;
2. ensure the effective implementation of the database that contains systematically gathered and regularly updated information relevant to gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation; and
3. evaluate thoroughly the data gathered from the database to come up with gender-responsive and gender-sensitive plans and programs which are in accordance with the GAD mandate.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed during exit conference that all department heads concern shall initiate the liquidation, reporting and be the focal person in case funds from the NGAs will again be downloaded to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the concerned to:
1. the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC), in coordination with the GAD Focal Point, to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database: manual or electronic, consisting of sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information;
2. ensure the effective implementation of the database that contains systematically gathered and regularly updated information relevant to gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation; and
3. evaluate thoroughly the data gathered from the database to come up with gender-responsive and gender-sensitive plans and programs which are in accordance with the GAD mandate.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not provide reason for non-implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality duly consider the instructive guidelines on the creation or establishment of the local economic enterprise provided in the Manual on Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises for Local Government Units 2016 developed and issued by the Department of Budget and Management. The local economic enterprise shall be created or established through an ordinance. It also particularly emphasizes the effective local economic enterprise that contributes the quality service delivery goals of the LGU. As such, it must be designed, set up, operated, and managed in accordance with a well-prepared feasibility study and a detailed business plan.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not provide comment on this observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned department heads to immediately initiate action to enforce settlement of the disallowances in accordance with the COA Revised Rules of Procedure on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA). Secure documents such as the Notice of Finality of Decision/COA order of Execution to trace back those disallowances reflected in the Receivables– Disallowances/Charges account. Once the persons liable and their accountability have been established issue demand letter to notify them of their liability and to enforce collection on them.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor requested the concerned department heads to initiate action on the collection of disallowances as per RRSA. The Municipal Administrator reported they will follow up with the Sangguniang Bayan if they will be allowed to write off certain dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality consider the instructive guidelines on the creation/establishment of local economic enterprises provided in the DBM Manual. The enterprise should be established through ordinance, founded on proper feasibility study and business plan.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not provide comment on this observation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to expedite the submission of the request for write-off of these dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit and provide supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will initiate letter request to the Commission on Audit for write off these undocumented and uncollected receivables. The Municipal Administrator averred that he will follow up with the Sangguniang Bayan if they will be allowed to write off the receivables to reconcile the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection/settlement of the disallowances as per RRSA and to record the audit disallowances of P4,117,000.00 as Receivables–Disallowances/Charges with subsidiary records including reference ND and liable persons, and include disclosures in the NFS regarding the audit disallowances of P3,654,662.51 that is subject of a recently-submitted request for reconsideration to COA RO IV.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant said he will follow-up and initiate actions, but no final action reported.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned to recompense the total disallowance in the amount of P1,271,804.84 through collection from the former mayor as the salary and benefits paid from the time they were appointed shall be the personal liability of former Mayor Rosario M. Apacible, pursuant to CSC Decision 170088.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has not yet provided a formal response; the concerned persons have not yet refunded the amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate and secure a copy of the approved guidelines for the grant of PBB from DILG and submit the same to COA for evaluation; otherwise cause the refund of the amount paid to its officials and employees amounting to P5,935,000.00. Failure will render the amount personal liability.
observations.managementAction: The Management did not comment; however it was noted as an issue to be addressed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan and Municipal Budget Officer consider for preparation and approval of Annual Budget the mandatory obligations such as electric utilities based on average monthly consumption and limit other expenses within the available appropriation in order to avoid disallowance in audit. Require all concerned officers to refund the amount of P2,810,062.65 for disbursements without appropriations.
observations.managementAction: The Management has not refunded the amount as of reporting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a group headed by the Human Resource Officer to undertake a thorough study and evaluation on actual workloads and manpower requirements for each department/offices to avoid unnecessary jobs and minimize government spending.
observations.managementAction: No definitive action reported; Management to study and evaluate manpower requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant observe provisions on the grant of transportation allowance and require refund of overpayment amounting to P101,250.00.
observations.managementAction: No refund reported; management to follow up.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause immediate enforcement and settlement of disallowances, record audit disallowances properly, and pursue settlement procedures per RRSA.
observations.managementAction: Actions to enforce collection were initiated, but unsettled balances remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to enforce settlement of the disallowances in accordance with RRSA and secure documentation for write-offs if applicable.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will initiate follow-ups and prepare necessary documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Inventory Committee headed by the General Services Officer to continue the
conduct of physical count of PPE in order to determine the existence of the
recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the results of the count with the
accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies, whether items
have already been missing, donated, sold and unserviceable to arrive at the
correct balance in the financial statements. As to the infrastructure assets,
coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to verify their existence, and prepare and
maintain the required Property Cards and update ARE to facilitate the
reconciliation of RPCPPE with the accounting records;
b. the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the General Services Officer to
clearly identify the items under Replacement Costing and Labor Payroll,
amounting to P81,384,996.97 as to what specific PPE items or accounts they were
attributed; and
c. the General Services Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to
consider the remedy provided in COA Circular No. 2020-06, dated January 31,
2020 to assist in coming up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and GSO (i) implement the perpetual
inventory method for accounting the supplies and inventories, (ii) maintain subsidiary
records for inventory in the form of supplies ledger cards and stock cards, respectively,
and (iii) ensure regular reconciliation of the accounting and property records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to exhaust
all efforts and devise strategies to collect all delinquent real property taxes and
encourage the tax payer to pay their dues on time, and likewise coordinate with the Liga
ng mga Barangay to facilitate the identification and serving of Notice of Delinquency to
concerned taxpayers.

Foregoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the members
of the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) and Municipal Engineer to
strictly monitor the implementation of the projects under 20% Development Fund; and
ensure optimal and timely utilization in the succeeding periods by including
development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready
pursuant to DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to revise
the LDRRMFIP of the Municipality for CY 2021 by including the financial
assistance received from National Government last CY 2020 and 2021 and
submit the same to the Audit Team;
b. require the Municipal Engineer to submit the status of the implementation of the
projects and the Municipal Accountant to facilitate immediate disbursement of
the financial assistance received if already completed subject to usual accounting
and auditing rules and regulations;
c. ensure that the LDRRMF be strictly utilized for the implementation of projects
and activities for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and
rehabilitation and recovery purposes; and
d. instruct the Local Finance Committee, namely: the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Budget Officer as well as the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Engineer,
and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to prepare the required
monthly and other quarterly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of
Program/Project/Activity Implementation and submit the same to the NDRRM
Council and other oversight bodies, with a copy furnished to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-MENRO to expedite the
processing of the required permits and certification to able to operate the alternative
technology and likewise instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide funds for the
processing of the required permits and certification.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct all department heads to
be strict in all its disbursements and expenses and ensure that all expenses are backed
up with appropriation ordinance.

With these, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG):
a. to scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower on all the projects to
be awarded, not on an individual basis only in cases where the latter is the lowest
bidder in several projects which have similar or overlapping implementation period,
in order to oblige the winning contractor to supplement its manpower to be utilized
in the projects awarded to it or to disqualify it from obtaining several awards or
contracts in conformity with the provisions of Section 34.1 and 34.3 of the Revised
IRR of RA 9184; and
b. to ensure that pertinent provisions of IRR of RA 9184 specifically in the posting of
NOA in the PhilGEPS, issuance of Notice to Proceed and posting of Performance
Security are strictly adhered to promote transparency and economy of its
procurement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to
strictly implement the aforementioned guidelines in order to avoid disallowance in audit.
Require all other concerned officers to refund the amount of P1,077,777.00 for
disbursements without appropriations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct Municipal Accountant and Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the lacking documents noted above or provide
justification for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRMO to verify the
individuals whose name were not listed in the master list of COVID 19 patients but were
being quarantined in the isolation facility and submit the same to the Audit Team for
further verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all department heads concern to
provide justification why these deficiencies on the documentation happened.

We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to check the legality and veracity of
the attached supporting documents before signing the disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a Memorandum/Order designating the
focal person/Officer that would be responsible for the uploading of the monthly reports
and coordination relative to the use of the COA Portal within the deadline set by COA
Circular No. 2021-02 and require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the required
reports for submission to the COA Portal and Audit Team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to investigate the abovementioned
transaction and submit report to the Commission on Audit after due deliberation, as well
as make necessary actions and precautions to avoid the occurrence of the same in the
future transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the liquidation reports if not yet
submitted and check to the source agency for refund of the unexpended balance;
and
b. The Head of the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate
with the concerned department heads to enforce the utilization and
implementation of those projects that were not yet implemented while pursue the
continuation of those ongoing utilization. Likewise, see to it that their respective
time frames and schedules are strictly monitored and evaluated to avoid delays
in its implementation.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned
Department Heads/employees to submit detailed action plans on the CY 2020 AAR
findings and recommendations, including that of prior years, to be incorporated in the
AAPSI, and to submit the AAPSI to the Audit Team. Likewise, assign a focal person who
will prepare the AAPSI and monitor the compliance of each concerned Department
Head/employee regarding the submission of detailed action plans.

Moreover, we recommended that the management follow strictly the above provisions
of the GAA and COA Memorandum No. 2014-002 on the submission of the AAPSI within
60 days from the receipt of the COA Annual Audit report to give enough time for the
Auditor to review, monitor and evaluate the action taken by the management on the
audit recommendations.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Inventory Committee headed by the General Services Officer to continue the conduct of physical count of PPE in order to determine the existence of the recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies, whether items have already been missing, donated, sold and unserviceable to arrive at the correct balance in the financial statements. As to the infrastructure assets, coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to verify their existence, and prepare and maintain the required Property Cards and update ARE to facilitate the reconciliation of RPCPPE with the accounting records;
b. the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the General Services Officer to clearly identify the items under Replacement Costing and Labor Payroll, amounting to P81,384,996.97 as to what specific PPE items or accounts they were attributed; and
c. the General Services Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to consider the remedy provided in COA Circular No. 2020-06, dated January 31, 2020 to assist in coming up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.managementAction: As per discussion during exit conference, the Management was amenable with the recommendations and planning to hold discussions with the concerned department heads to adapt the one time cleansing of PPE.
observations.reason: The Management reasoned out that they were doing their best to complete the physical inventory however it was delayed due to their other functions.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to come up with a corrected Report of Delinquent Accounts as of December 31, 2020 and reconcile their records with the Municipal Accountant to present a reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The balances of these accounts as presented in the financial statements were already reconciled. Likewise, the Report of Delinquency was reconciled to the balances of RPT and RPT SET Receivables at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the stale checks and the Municipal Accountant make corresponding adjustments in the municipality’s books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Bank Reconciliation Statements showed no stale checks in all its depository banks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. GSO to (i) prepare the stock cards for the purchased supplies; (ii) conduct periodic inventory of supplies; (iii) prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Inventories and submit the same to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the reported inventory balances in the books; (iv) prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the RSMI for issued inventories and henceforth, prepare the RSMI on a monthly basis; and (v) support issuance of inventories with properly approved Requisition Issue Slips;
b. Municipal Accountant to (i) record the purchases under the proper Inventory account as Asset instead of as Expenses; and (ii) maintain supplies ledger cards for all inventory accounts to support the recorded balances of inventory.
We also recommended that the (c) Municipal Administrator, when the budget permits, to (i) consider the provision of a storage room for the procurement of supplies in significant quantities; and (ii) assign a storekeeper to effectively discharge property accountability and maintenance of records and reports.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO reasoned out that they were working hard to complete physical counts of all its inventory accounts however due to limited time and manpower the same was not completed.
The Municipal Accountant reasoned out that he lacked personnel who will prepare and maintain supplies ledger cards.
The GSO reasoned out that the Office of the GSO is also the storage room. They were already monitoring the issuances and requisitions of office supplies.
observations.reason: GSO reasoned out that they were working hard to complete the physical counts but due to limited time and manpower the same was not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly read and follow the guidelines set therein, especially on posting the Notice of Award, Contract and Purchase Order on the website of the Procuring Entity (PE) or if none, any conspicuous place in the premises of the Procuring Entity and the GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement. Likewise, submit justification on why the procedures were omitted. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MSWDO to submit an explanation letter and a status report for the 1,018 unclaimed or returned Relief Goods. Require the MSWDO to submit the List of Recipients with supporting documents and explain the discrepancies.
observations.managementAction:
The Management submitted justification and the Audit Team acknowledged the submission.
The Local Chief Executive instructed the MSWDO to submit explanation letter and status report. Demand Letters were submitted and acknowledged by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
a. include in their plans and programs projects that have an impact on health and environment to control the spread of the virus;
b. afford additional allocation for PPAs for Covid-19 preventive measures in accordance with Item 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and the 20% DF; and
c. continue to be compliant in the preparation of COVID-related PPAs and assure inclusion thereof in the AIP in accordance with DBM DILG expenses not to realign its 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management averred and the Audit Team acknowledged that they had enough funds under LDRRMF for COVID 19 pandemic. The Management allocated PPAs for COVID related expenses under their LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LDRRMO to:
a. properly prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP indicating the (i) detailed PPAs and the corresponding appropriation classified as either MOOE or CO and (ii) under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years for ease of monitoring of project implementation and evaluation;
b. ensure that the LDRRMF be strictly utilized for the implementation of their projects and activities for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery purposes; and
c. the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the LDRRMO to comply with the monthly submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.
We likewise recommended that the amount inappropriately charged against LDRRMF be restored to LDRMMF.
observations.managementAction:
The Management fully complied with the monthly submission of Reports. The LDRRMO will revise the 2020 and 2021 LDRRMFIP as recommended reflecting LBC 130 and LBC 134 financial assistance.
The Management commented that it would be difficult to restore the amount because the other PPAs as scheduled will be sacrificed.
observations.reason: The Management reasoned that it would be difficult to restore the amount because other PPAs would be sacrificed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as chairperson of the LCPC, enjoin the members of the Council to adhere to Rule 15.b of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9344 that the Municipality shall allocate the minimum one percent (1%) of its IRA and utilize the same for LCPC programs and activities. Also require that the LCPC utilize the fund for the benefit of the students and comply with the provisions of the joint circulars.
observations.managementAction: The Management is amenable with the recommendation and will comply accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the MSWDO to in coordination with barangay officials maintain/update profile of families under its jurisdiction; together with the Municipal Treasurer send demand letters in coordination with the barangay officials to those SAP beneficiaries who received duplicate assistance; coordinate with the OIC-Regional Director of DSWD RO IV-A for clarification of actions to be taken on the SAP beneficiaries with duplication of assistance; and explain and submit documents for the one SAP beneficiary who did not sign in the payroll and no documents attached to signify receipt of the same.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted explanation letter to the Audit Team. The OIC-Regional Director of DSWD RO IV-A was requested for clarification. Demand Letters were already sent in coordination with the barangay officials. Justification letter was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the concerned to ensure compliance: a. Municipal Planning and Development Officer to integrate to the overall project cost Pay Item “SPL- Construction Safety and Health” a lump sum amount to be quantified in the detailed estimate to ensure the occupational safety and health condition of workers and the public in the project site/location; b. Municipal Accountant to require the approved CSHP prior to processing of contractor’s claims either for advance payment or first progress billing.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer justified that they could not monitor all contractors at once hence the line item was omitted. The Municipal Accountant already required the said document before payment had been made.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer justified that they could not monitor all contractors at once hence the line item was omitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to comply with the requirements provided in GPPB Circular No. 02-2020 by (a) posting the procurement reports such as the APP, PMRs and APCI in the website of the Procuring Entity or any conspicuous place and the GPPB online portal and (b) submitting to the GPPB the approved APPs, PMRs and APCPI Results through the electronic mail address. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the IT Officer to set-up, administer and maintain the official website of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Budgetary constraints. The Management assured they will start posting procurement notices and the IT Officer will set up the website.
observations.reason: Budgetary constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer exercise prudence in the preparation of work program of infrastructure projects to ensure efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the implementation of infrastructure projects. Also require the Municipal Engineer to submit additional justification and explanation for the COA Technical Audit Specialists' findings; otherwise the amount will be subject for issuance of Notice of Disallowance and refund.
observations.managementAction: Documents were submitted to COA Technical Audit Specialist.
observations.reason: Request for recalculation was submitted to COA Technical Audit Specialist.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the bidding process, especially in the selection of eligible contractors. Furthermore, careful study and proper planning should be observed prior to any procurement process to prevent untoward delays. Submit an explanation letter on the noted lapses in the bidding process.
observations.managementAction: The project involved the past administration and the Municipality is addressing the issues as necessary.
observations.reason: The project involves the past administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO to submit explanation letter why they ought to purchase not from the supplier who offered the lowest quotation. Likewise, explain the necessity of the specific laptop model purchased, and provide supporting documents for evaluation by the Audit Team. Otherwise, such transaction shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The GSO already submitted explanation letter to the Audit Team as to the purchase of the laptop and its necessity.
observations.reason: The Management did not submit additional documentation on the necessity of the purchase.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Human Resource Management Officer to explain the inconsistencies and provide additional documents to prove the propriety of the claim. Otherwise excess payments will be subject to audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO explained there was agreement between the personnel involved on the payment of the Hazard Pay. Additional documents were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the members of the Local School Board to utilize fully the Special Education Fund for the benefit of the students considering the programs/projects enumerated in Section 272 of RA 7160 implemented by DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circulars No. 01 s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017. Likewise, refrain from charging non-priority expenses from the Special Education Fund and strictly comply with the provisions of the above-stated regulations; otherwise, such transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit if incurred repeatedly.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied and presented justifications that the expenditures were properly used for allowable purposes.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned Department Heads/employees to submit detailed action plans on the CY 2019 AAR findings and recommendations, including that of prior years, to be incorporated in the AAPSI, and to submit the AAPSI to the Audit Team. Likewise, assign a focal person who will prepare the AAPSI and monitor the compliance of each concerned Department Head/employee regarding the submission of detailed action plans. Follow strictly the provisions on the submission of the AAPSI within 60 days from the receipt of the COA Annual Audit report.
observations.managementAction: The Management is amenable with the recommendation and will comply accordingly.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
1. The BPLO, in coordination with the Market Administrator to require stallholders to obtain their business permit;
2. The BPLO to strategize the tightening of the enforcement of the Local Revenue Code to ensure that all businesses operating within the Municipality have secured their business permit; and
3. The BPLO to recompute the unpaid taxes of the business taxpayers corresponding to the understatement of gross receipts, proceed with the collection thereof, and submit to the Audit Team the results of the actions taken.
observations.managementAction: The BPLO intensifies its operation requiring all stall holders to secure their business permits. The BPLO already sent letters to the delinquent taxpayers. The BPLO computed for the penalties in business permits.
observations.reason: The BPLO noted that pandemic and lack of manpower hindered full enforcement and collection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all department heads concerned to resolve all the issues hindering the full implementation of the computerization project. The Municipal Planning and Development Office to expedite the finalization and encoding of the Annual Investment Plan for CY 2021 to permit other departments to proceed with their usage of the system.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered with the concern and informed that the computerization is not yet fully operational due to data build up; however, they are working on it. The Municipal Planning and Development Officer expedited encoding for CY 2022.
observations.reason: The computerization is not yet fully operational due to data build-up and constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to: (a) strictly implement the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) with the detailed plan and corresponding cost estimate duly acknowledged by the EMB to ensure that the open dumpsite closure and rehabilitation are done with environmental protection measures; (b) submit to the Audit Team status and monitoring reports of Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan; and (c) expedite the compliance of the requested documents necessary for the approval of the 10-Year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan (2019-2028) by the NSWMC.
observations.managementAction: The request for the Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC) has already been applied. The department committed to secure all permits necessary for the operation of the alternative technology and solution in managing solid wastes in compliance to RA 9003.
observations.reason: Approval of the 10-year SWM Plan still pending due to absence of ECC and Permit to Operate requested documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to: (a) make a reassessment or investigation on the veracity of the reasons provided by the contractor for their request for the suspension of work and grant the said request only if there is a valid reason to suspend work; (b) require the contractor to complete the project as soon as possible and impose applicable sanctions and penalties if warranted; and (c) compute the amount of liquidated damages, if warranted, to be computed and deducted from the next payments to be made to the contractor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer made reassessment and reported that the project has been completed and delivered. Liquidated damages were computed and collected from the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned officials to ensure the annual preparation of the required reports, the conduct of inventory of roads and submission of the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Engineer reported that they were not able to implement the same due to the pandemic.
observations.reason: Pandemic hindered the conduct of inventories and preparation of reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and concerned officers: (a) exercise diligence in procurement planning and execution to ensure proper fiscal management; (b) conduct consultation, study and evaluation to consider feasibility and effectiveness; (c) require MENRO to submit justification on the non-utilization; (d) submit justification why another procurement was made despite prior audit observations; and (e) furnish COA the lacking documents.
observations.managementAction: The Management has not provided reasons. The MENRO and concerned offices will review utilization and maintenance of facilities.
observations.reason: No specific reason provided by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and officials strictly adhere to Section 325 PS cap limitation and refrain from incurring additional Personal Services and require refund of disallowed amount from approving authority and concerned officials/employees.
observations.managementAction: The incumbent Mayor adhered with the existing guidelines and there is an ongoing appeal with the Civil Service Commission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer require the contractor to submit the lacking documents so that a complete technical review and evaluation of the contracts and reported work accomplishments can be conducted by the COA Technical Audit Specialist; non-compliance would result to issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Documents were submitted to the previous Audit Team and COA Technical Audit Specialist.
observations.reason: Documents were submitted to previous Audit Team; full technical evaluation is pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to conduct consultation, study and evaluation of the project to avoid possible wastage of government funds; coordinate with barangay officials for strict implementation; consider provision of mini-garbage truck for each barangay to help increase frequency of garbage collection.
observations.managementAction: The MENRO is coordinating with barangay officials to improve utilization and maintenance. Some units were rehabilitated and are in use.
observations.reason: Some barangays are not properly maintaining the units; efforts ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Officials allocate funds for yearly registration and insurance and ensure recovery of costs from insurance in case of untoward incident.
observations.managementAction: The motor vehicles were already registered with LTO; some still lacked insurance coverage due to budget constraints.
observations.reason: Lack of budget for insurance coverage.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. the Inventory Committee headed by the General Services Officer to continue
the conduct of physical count of PPE in order to determine the existence of the
recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the results of the count with the
accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies, whether items
have already been missing, donated, sold and unserviceable to arrive at the
correct balance in the financial statements. As to the infrastructure assets,
coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to verify their existence, and prepare
and maintain the required Property Cards and update ARE to facilitate the
reconciliation of RPCPPE with the accounting records;

b. the Inventory Committee to properly utilize the required inventory reports
stated on the above-mentioned regulations;

c. the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the General Services Officer to
clearly identify the items under Replacement Costing and Labor Payroll,
amounting to ₱83,331,647.72 as to what specific PPE items or accounts they
were attributed; and

d. the General Services Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to
consider the remedy provided in COA Circular 2020-06, dated January 31, 2020 to
assist in coming up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition and accountability.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to come up with a corrected balance of its Report of Delinquent Accounts
as of December 31, 2020 and reconcile their records with the Municipal Accountant
to present a reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct:

a. the Municipal Accountant to record the Land in Barangay XI purchased in
the amount of ₱619,565.00 and adjust the Land account by the appraised value of
all the donated real properties. Reconcile Accounting with related records of the
landholdings of the municipality;

b. the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate in the ensuing years’ budget the cost for
titling of Land; and

c. all concerned department heads to coordinate with each other and exert extra
efforts to secure the documents relative to the purchase made from Mr. Reynaldo
Cargado. Work together towards the reconciliation and registration of all
landholdings to protect the municipality against undue risk of third party claim.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer effect the cancellation of the stale
checks and the Municipal Accountant make corresponding adjustments in the
municipality’s books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

a. GSO to (i) prepare the stock cards for the purchased supplies; (ii) conduct
periodic inventory of supplies; (iii) prepare the corresponding Report on the
Physical Count of Inventories and submit the same to the Accounting Office
for reconciliation with the reported inventory balances in the books; (iv)
prepare and submit to the Accounting Office the RSMI for issued inventories
and henceforth, prepare the RSMI on a monthly basis; and (v) support
issuance of inventories with properly approved Requisition Issue Slips;

b. Municipal Accountant to (i) record the purchases under the proper Inventory
account as Asset instead of as Expenses; and (ii) maintain supplies ledger
cards for all inventory accounts to support the recorded balances of inventory.
We also recommended that the (c) Municipal Administrator, when the budget permits,
to (i) consider the provision of a storage room for the procurement of supplies in
significant quantities; and (ii) assign a storekeeper to effectively discharge property
accountability and maintenance of records and reports.

For future transactions related to this, we recommended that Local Chief Executive
instruct the Bids and Awards Committee secretariat to strictly read and follow the
guidelines set therein, especially on posting the Notice of Award, Contract and
Purchase Order on the website of the Procuring Entity (PE) or if none, any
conspicuous place in the premises of the Procuring Entity and the GPPB online portal
for Emergency Procurement. Likewise, submit justification on why the procedures
were omitted.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MSWDO to submit an
explanation letter and a status report for the 1,018 unclaimed or returned Relief Goods.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the planning officer to:

a. include in their plans and programs projects that have an impact on health
and environment issues to control the spread of the virus;

b. afford additional allocation for PPAs for Covid-19 preventive measures in
accordance with Item 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, the 20% DF; and

c. continue to be compliant in the preparation of COVID-related PPAs and
assure inclusion thereof in the AIP in accordance with Section 2.1 DBM DILG
JMC No. 02.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:

a. the LDRRMO to properly prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) indicating the
(i) detailed PPAs and the corresponding appropriation classified as either
Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) or Capital Outlay (CO)
and (ii) under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to
the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years for ease of monitoring of project
implementation and evaluation;

b. ensure that the LDRRMF be strictly utilized for the implementation of projects
and activities for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response
and rehabilitation and recovery purposes; and

c. the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the LDRRMO to (i) comply with
the monthly submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

We likewise recommended that the amount inappropriately charged against LDRRMF be
restored to LDRMMF.

We recommended stringent compliance with the provision of Section 5(2) of the Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 of the Philippine
Commission on Women, Department of the Interior and Local Government,
Department of Budget and Management and National Economic and Development
Authority that the Municipality shall submit its Annual Gender and Development
(GAD) Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office
(PPDO) of the Provincial Government to ensure the alignment of the municipal GAD
PPAs with the priorities of the province and then to the DILG Provincial Office for its
review and endorsement to the concerned LGU for incorporation in its annual budget
to be enacted by the Local Sanggunian.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC, in coordination with the
GAD Focal Point, to:

a. spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database, manual or electronic,
consisting of sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information;

b. ensure the effective implementation of the database that contains
systematically gathered and regularly updated information relevant to
gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation; and

c. evaluate thoroughly the data gathered from the database to come up with
gender-responsive and gender-sensitive plans and programs which are in
accordance with the GAD mandate.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the chairperson of the Local
Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC), enjoin the members of the Council to
adhere to Rule 15.b of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No.
9344 or the “Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006” and to DILG Memorandum
Circular 2012-120 that mandate the LGU to strengthen and implement the programs,
projects and activities of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC).

To avoid duplication of assistance, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor enjoin
the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO):

a. in coordination with the barangay officials, to continuously work hand-in-
hand in maintaining/updating profile of families under its jurisdiction to
properly and readily identify the eligible beneficiaries of various
programs/projects which may be initiated either by government or private
sector;

b. together with the Municipal Treasurer to send demand letters in coordination
with the barangay officials to those SAP beneficiaries who received duplicate
assistance to exact payment;

c. to coordinate with the OIC-Regional Director of DSWD Regional Office IV-A
for the clarification of actions to be taken on the SAP beneficiaries with
duplication of assistance: and

d. explain and submit documents for the one SAP beneficiary who did not sign
in the payroll and no documents attached to signify receipt of the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:

a. Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to
integrate to the overall project cost under Pay Item “SPL-Construction Safety
and Health” a lump sum amount to be quantified in the detailed estimate to
ensure the occupational safety and health condition of workers and the
general public in the project site/ location; and

b. Municipal Accountant to require the approved Construction Safety and Health
Program (CSHP) prior to the processing of the contractor’s claim either for
advance payment or first progress billing.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards
Committee to comply with the requirements provided in GPPB Circular No. 02-2020
dated May 20, 2020 by (a) posting the procurement reports such as the Annual
Procurement Plan (APP), Procurement Monitoring Reports (PMRs) and Agency
Procurement Compliance and Performance Indicator (APCI) in the website of the
Procuring Entity (PE) or if none, in any conspicuous place in the premises of the
Procuring Entity and the GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement made within
the set schedule in order to promote transparency in the procurement process; and
b) submitting to the GPPB the approved APPs, PMRs and APCPI Results through the
electronic mail address.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Information
Technology Officer to set-up, administer and maintain the official website of the
Municipality to effectively promote transparency and accountability of the procurement
processes and activities of the agency.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor enjoin the Municipal Engineer to exercise
prudence in the preparation of work program of infrastructure projects to ensure
efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the implementation of infrastructure
projects.

Also, we further recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit additional
justification and explanation for the deficiencies found on the two infrastructure
projects in order for the Audit Team to elevate the matter to COA Region IV-A
Technical Audit Specialists; otherwise, it will be subject for issuance of Notice of
Disallowance and will result in the refund of ₱129,433.69.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly adhere to the bidding process,
esspecially in the selection of eligible contractors. Furthermore, careful study and proper
planning should be observed prior to any procurement process to prevent untoward delays
to the detriment of their constituents. Likewise, submit an explanation letter on the noted
lapses on the bidding process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO to submit explanation
letter why they ought to purchase not from the supplier who offered the lowest
quotation. Likewise, explain the necessity of the specific laptop model purchased,
subject to evaluation of the Audit Team. Otherwise, such transaction shall be
disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Human Resource and
Management Officer to explain the aforementioned inconsistencies. Likewise, provide
additional documentation to prove the propriety of the claim for COVID-19 Hazard
Pay; otherwise, excess payments will be subject for audit disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the members of the Local School
Board to utilize fully the Special Education Fund for the benefit of the students
considering the programs/projects enumerated in Section 272 of Republic Act 7160
as implemented by DedEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circulars No. 01 s. 2017 dated
January 19, 2017. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipality refrain from charging
non-priority expenses from the Special Education Fund and strictly comply with the
provisions of the above-stated regulations; otherwise, such transactions will be
suspended or disallowed in audit if incurred repeatedly.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the department
head/s or personnel concerned to comply with the completion and submission to the
Audit Team of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for part
III of CY 2020 AAR and on every AAR thereafter.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
1. The BPLO, in coordination with the Market Administrator to require
stallholders to obtain their business permit;
2. The BPLO to strategize the tightening of the enforcement of the Local Revenue
Code to ensure that all businesses operating within the Municipality have
secured their business permit; and
3. The BPLO, to recompute the unpaid taxes of the business-taxpayers
corresponding to the understatement of gross receipts, proceed with the
collection thereof, and submit to the Audit Team the results of the actions
taken.

We also recommended that the Management submit justification letter to explain the
reason for their failure to comply with the prior year audit recommendations.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that:
a) The General Services Officer submit to the Municipal Accountant the set of papers
pertaining to the disposal of unserviceable properties for deduction from the PPE
account;
b) Require person responsible/accountable to request for relief from accountability for
PPE that had been lost are being required to request for relief from the Commission;
c) The Municipal Accountant reclassify the proper PPE accounts in the books and make
the necessary adjusting entries to present the correct balances.

assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection therein. Also, coordinate
with the Office of the Provincial Treasurer for more issuance of Warrant of Levy;
b) Ensure that the Sangguniang Bayan enact ordinance that will enhance collection
scheme, encourage taxpayers to pay their taxes and impose additional penalties or
incentives to promote sound collection system as provided on existing provisions of
the Local Government Code; and
c) Maximize availment of all legal and administrative remedies either by administrative
levy on real property or judicial action.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct that:

a. the Inventory Committee headed by the General Services Officer prepare the
inventory list by category and perform the actual and complete physical count of PPE
in order to determine the existence of the recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the
results of the count with the accounting records and establish the causes of
discrepancies, whether items have already been missing, donated, sold and
unserviceable to arrive at the correct balance in the financial statements. As to the
infrastructure assets, coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to verify their existence,
prepare and maintain the required Property Cards and update ARE to facilitate the
reconciliation of RPCPPE with the accounting records;

b. the Accounting Department exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPEs included in
the beginning/unidentified balances; secure the necessary documents to support
those PPEs to be able to reconcile with the Inventory Report of the GSO; obtain
proper documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous
recording or for inclusion to ensure that the amounts are valid; and conform to the
rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments; and

c. the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer maintain complete and
updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the
reconciliation of both records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to come up
with a corrected balance of its Report of Delinquent Accounts as of December 31,
2019 and reconcile their records with the Municipal Accountant to present a reliable RPT
and SET Receivables in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairman in coordination
with the BAC TWG to explain the observed inconsistencies in the procurement process
and to immediately submit the aforementioned lacking documentsand other relevant
supporting documents/reports to facilitate further review and evaluation by the Audit
Team and the COA Information and Technology Audit Office.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. all department heads concerned to resolve all the issues hindering the full
implementation of the computerization project, discuss them with the supplier, and
come up with a favorable solution.

b. the Municipal Planning and Development Office to expedite the finalization and
encoding of the Annual Investment Plan for CY 2021 to permit other departments to
proceed with their usage of the system.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Accounting Office to analyze the
accounts with unremitted balances and ensure that the responsible
officials/employees to remit immediately the withheld funds to BIR, GSIS, Philhealth,
and PAG-IBIG.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the liquidation reports, if not yet
submitted, and coordinate with the source agency for refund of the unexpended
balance; and

b. the Head of the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with the
concerned department heads to expedite the pending implementation of projects and
pursue the completion of projects on-going utilization. Likewise, see to it that future
fund transfers are fully accomplished within the time frame and refund immediately to
the source agency any balance accruing to them.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. The BPLO, in coordination with the Market Specialist to require stallholders to
obtain their business permit.

b. The BPLO to strategize the tightening of the enforcement of the Local Revenue
Code to ensure that all businesses operating within the Municipality have secured
their business permit.

c. The BPLO, to recompute the unpaid taxes of the business-taxpayers
corresponding to the understatement of gross receipts, proceed with the
collection thereof, and submit to the Audit Team the results of the actions taken.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the municipal officials
concerned to undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual
budget on the basis of reasonable and/or realistic projection of revenues for the
ensuing year, in accordance with the intent of local government budgeting that will
support the proper allocation of expenditures as provided under Section 316 of RA
7160. Moreso, exert tremendous efforts to attain the targeted revenue for the benefit
of its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO:

a. to strictly implement the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) with the
detailed plan and corresponding cost estimate that was duly acknowledged by the
Environment and Management Bureau (EMB) to ensure that the open
dumpsite closure and rehabilitation are done with environmental protection measures
and safeguards in place;

b. to submit to the Audit Team status and monitoring reports of the Safe Closure and
Rehabilitation Plan; and

c. to expedite the compliance of the requested documents necessary for the approval
of the 10-Year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan (2019-2028) by the National
Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. the BAC Chairman, in coordination with the BAC-TWG, to meticulously conduct
the evaluation of bids and post-qualification in order to ascertain that the winning
bidders can comply with the terms and conditions of the contracts and ensure
that the personnel and equipment of the contractors with simultaneous or
overlapping periods differ from each other;

b. the BAC, if it verifies any of the deficiencies enumerated in Section 34.3 of the
Revised IRR of Republic Act 9184 to be due to the contractor’s fault or negligence,
to disqualify the contractor from the award for the procurement of Infrastructure
Projects;

c. the Municipal Engineer to monitor the project implementation of the Municipality
in accordance with functions cited in Section 477 (b) (3) of RA 7160 and ensure the
timely completion thereof. Likewise, address immediately the incidence of negative
slippage to avoid further damages to the government and its constituents; and

d. the BAC Chairman and the Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate in the
evaluation of contractors, particularly on their performance of work.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local School Board to submit
the School Improvement Plan and the Annual Implementation Plan and refrain from
approving the Special Education Fund Annual Budget without these supporting
documents to ensure that the Special Education Fund (SEF) will be utilized for the
intended purpose or according to the identified priority areas listed in the Annual
Implementation Plan (AIP) culled from the DepEd-approved School Improvement Plan
(SIP).

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality refrain from charging non-priority
expenses from the Special Education Fund and strictly comply with the provisions of the
above-stated regulations; otherwise, such transactions will be suspended or disallowed
in audit if incurred repeatedly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the chairperson of the Local
Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC), enjoin the members of the Council to
adhere to the provisions of Rule 15.b of Rules and Regulations Implementing
Republic Act No. 9344 or the “Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006” and toDILG
Memorandum Circular 2012-120 that mandate the LGU to strengthen and implement
the programs, projects and activities of the Local Councils for the Protection of
Children (LCPC).

We recommended stringent compliance with the provision of Section 5(2) of the Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 of the Philippine
Commission on Women, Department of the Interior and Local Government,
Department of Budget and Management and National Economic and Development
Authority that the Municipality shall submit her Annual Gender and Development
(GAD) Plan and Budget (GPB) to the Provincial Planning and Development Office
(PPDO) of the Provincial Government to ensure the alignment of the municipal GAD
PPAs with the priorities of the province and then to the DILG Provincial Office for its
review and endorsement to the concerned LGU for incorporation in her annual
budget to be enacted by her Local Sanggunian.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MPDC in coordination with the
GAD Focal Point:

a. to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database, manual or electronic,
consisting of sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information;

b. to ensure the effective implementation of the database that contains
systematically gathered and regularly updated information relevant to gender-
responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation; and

c. to evaluate thoroughly the data gathered from the database to come up with
gender-responsive and gender-sensitive plans and programs which are in
accordance with the GAD mandate.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to ensure the
efficient and effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund which is
primarily intended to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of
desirable socio-economic development;

b. the Management to strictly observe Article 454 (d) of the Implementing Rules
and Regulations of RA 7160, which states that disbursements from special
accounts shall proceed from itemized appropriation which shall be for specific
development projects/activities embodied in the local development plan and/or
public investment program formulated and prioritized by the Local
Development Council and approved by the Sanggunian concerned.

We likewise recommended that changes in the Annual Budget be implemented in
accordance with Article 417 and 454 (b) of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of
RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer:

a. to make a reassessment or investigation on the veracity of the reasons provided by
the contractor for their request for the suspension of work and grant the said request
only if there isa valid reason to suspend work per Government Procurement Manual
Volume 3;

b. to require the contractor to complete the project as soon as possible and impose
applicable sanctions and penalties if warranted, in accordance with Sections 8 of
Annex E (Contract Implementation Guidelines for the Procurement of Infrastructure
Projects) of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA 9184; and

c. to compute the amount of liquidated damages, if warranted, to be deducted from
the next payments to be made to the contractor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council to include in the
LDRRMP the PPAs within the DRRM fund in compliance with the provisions
under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

b. The LDRRMP should be reviewed annually to include the activities to be funded
from the unexpended 30% QRF and 70% DRRMF-MOOE of the preceding year.

c. Refrain from using DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not directly
related to disaster preparedness, mitigation, response and rescue operations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned officials to
ensure the annual preparation of the required reports, the conduct of inventory of
roads and submission of the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed
period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer to
inform the COA office for the deliveries of procured equipment, goods and materials for
proper inspection of the items within 24 hours as provided under COA Circular No.
2009-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct that:
a. the Inventory Committee headed by the General Services Officer prepare the
inventory list by category and perform the actual and complete physical count of PPE
in order to determine the existence of the recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the
results of the count with the accounting records and establish the causes of
discrepancies, whether items have already been missing, donated, sold and
unserviceable to arrive at the correct balance in the financial statements. As to the
infrastructure assets, coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to verify their existence,
prepare and maintain the required Property Cards and update ARE to facilitate the
reconciliation of RPCPPE with the accounting records;
b. the Accounting Department exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPEs included in
the beginning/unidentified balances; secure the necessary documents to support
those PPEs to be able to reconcile with the Inventory Report of the GSO; obtain
proper documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous
recording or for inclusion to ensure that the amounts are valid; and conform to the
rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments; and
c. the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer maintain complete and
updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the
reconciliation of both records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Accounting Office to analyze
the accounts with unremitted balances and ensure that the responsible
officials/employees to remit immediately the withheld funds to BIR, GSIS, Philhealth,
and PAG-IBIG.
observations.managementAction:
Managements’ Comments:
“This is in relation to your Audit Observation Memorandum No. 2020-008 (19) which I
received last September 17, 2020 requesting my comments on the foregoing audit
observations. The said AOM mainly refers to mandatory remittances Due to BIR, GSIS,
PHILHEALTH and PAG-IBIG Fund. Upon receiving the said AOM, I coordinated with the
Municipal Accountant on the matter and we agreed that he will be the one to give his
comments on the matters regarding BIR, PHILHEALTH AND PAG-IBIG since the Office of
the Municipal Accountant is the office in charge of remitting the said mandatory deductions
and has more understanding on the reasons or explanations for the listed under remittance.
With regards to the remittance of Due to GSIS, the under remittance normally
happens because the basis of amounts to be deducted which from the GSIS Electronic
Billing File for a particular month is always sent a month earlier which is based on the
current salaries and loans of our employees for that period. By the time of the actual date of
remittance, some employees have already renewed their loans which means that the
outstanding balance for that particular loan have already been deducted by GSIS from the
gross loanable amount. Because of this, the Accounting Office will no longer deduct the
monthly amortization for that particular month because that amount is already included in
the outstanding balance which was deducted from the gross loanable amount which
resulted in the net loanable amount of those employees. Another reason which contributes
to this is when employees are on leave without pay, resign, retire or is separated from the
service, the amount that was billed is based on the whole basic monthly salary but the
amount that would actually be remitted will be based on the prorated amount based on the
actual salary received for the month depending on the effectivity of which.
We would like to express our gratitude for your observations recommendations,
please be assured we are closely coordinating with GSIS with regards to the timely reporting
and updating of records of our employees in order to avoid if not minimize the variance in
billing and actual remittance of the said deductions.”

b. to submit to the Audit Team status and monitoring reports of the Safe Closure

approval of the 10-Year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan (2019-2028)
by the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWMC).
observations.managementAction:
Managements’ Comments:
“As compliance to RA 9003, the Municipality is adopting an alternative technology
instead of sanitary landfill for its final disposal facility which is stated in the 10 year solid
waste management plan. Previously, the alternative technology is expected to be operating
in the first quarter of the year 2020, but due to pandemic it is delayed. At this time, only two
(2) equipment are lacking for the full operation of the technology. Hopefully, it will be
operational by the last quarter of 2020.
While the alternative technology is under construction, the Municipality is in contract with
Bauan Sanitary Land Fill for the final disposal of the residuals. Prior to disposal, the wastes
are being contained in the Residual Containment Area (RCA) located also in the site.
Hazardous / Special Wastes are being contained in the septic vault and biodegradables are
left in every household.
Unfortunately, this set up did not continue due to the closure of Bauan Sanitary Land
Fill in this time of pandemic. This is the reason of the partial re-opening of the site. But,
every dump is being covered by soil.
As to the pending approval of the 10 year Solid Waste Management Plan, we have
already submitted the required documents to the National Solid Waste Management
Commission.
We have already furnished your office copies of the following documents on
September 14, 2020
- 10 Year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan
- Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan
- DOST Communications
As to the implementation of Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP)”
1. Fence had been constructed
2. Bill Board has been installed
3. The disposal site had been planted with caballero trees and bougainvilla and san
Francisco but did not prosper
4. Septic Vault has been constructed”
observations.reason: See Finding No. 5 in Part 2 of this report

observations.recommendation:
• The Inventory Committee headed by the General Services Officer prepare inventory
list by category and perform the actual and complete physical count of PPE in order
to determine the existence of the recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the results of
the count with the accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies,
whether items have already been missing, donated, sold and unserviceable to arrive
at the correct balance in the financial statements. As to the infrastructure assets,
coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to verify their existence, prepare and
maintain the required Property Cards and update ARE to facilitate the reconciliation
of RPCPPE with the accounting records;
• The Accounting Department exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPEs included in
the beginning/unidentified balances. Secure the necessary documents to support

proper documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous
