rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments; and
• The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer maintain complete and
updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the
reconciliation of both records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
• Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their records to
present a reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements; and
• Municipal Treasurer exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all
delinquent real property taxes and encourage the tax payer to pay their dues on
time. Conduct verification on those outstanding balances to determine the
accuracy of the receivables and its collectability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and his
staff to coordinate again with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile their records
on receivables/payables and their corresponding payables/receivables in the books of the
Province to establish the correct balances of the inter-agency accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review in detail all entries made on
the accounts Due and Subsidy from/to Other Funds in the General Fund, Special
Education Fund and Trust Fund and check any unrecorded and misrecorded
transaction/s in its reciprocal account/s. Draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to
make necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of the accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
• The Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the liquidation reports if not yet
submitted and the check to the source agency for refund of the unexpended
balance. See to it that future fund transfers are fully implemented within the time
frame to avoid delay in liquidation; and
• The Head of the Municipal Planning and Development Office to coordinate with
the concerned department heads to enforce the utilization and implementation of
those projects that were not yet implemented while pursue the continuation of
those ongoing utilization. Likewise, see to it that their respective time frames and
schedules are strictly monitored and evaluated to avoid delays in its
implementation.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
• BAC-TWG conduct extensive procedures during the evaluation of bids and post-
qualification in order to ascertain that the winning bidders can comply with the
terms and conditions of the contracts as agreed upon; and
• Municipal Engineer to monitor the simultaneous implementation projects by the
same contractor to guard against unwarranted delays or substandard quality of
work

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned municipal officials
ensure the full, optimal and effective utilization of the 20% Development Fund which
is primarily intended to fund projects that shall contribute to the attainment of
desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of
the concerned LGU for the benefits of its constituents. We further recommended
that the development projects/activities shall be shown in itemized appropriations in
the municipal budget and the supplemental annual investment program shall be
prepared and approved for any programs/projects/activities to be included in the
annual or supplemental budget as well as any changes shall be made pursuant to
Sections 321 and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Articles 417 and 454(b) of the Act
Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with Sections 3 of Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 of Philippine Commission on Women (PCW),
Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG), Department of Budget and
Management (DBM), and National Economic Development Authority (NEDA) dated
January 12, 2016 in the attribution of a portion or the total budget of mainstreamed
PPAs to the GAD budget.

Based on the forgoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRM)
to enforce the implementation of the programs and projects of the Municipality
under 70% Mitigation Fund and have those programs completed within the
intended time to avoid delay in the delivery of services and benefits to the
constituents;
• Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRM)
to ensure that the future programs and activities charged to the 5% LDRRMF are
aligned to the Disaster Prevention and Preparedness goals of the Municipality.
Likewise, direct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for the MOOE
and Capital Outlay of the MDRRM Office to avoid charging non-priority
expenditures to the calamity fund;
• Require LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through LDRRMC and Local
Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002.
Also, coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant
to prepare the report in accordance with the prescribed format and have the
amounts reconciled with their records; and
• Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial
Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF as well
as the other required disclosure in order to inform the users of relevant
information.

For the foregoing deficiencies, we recommended the following:
a. That the Local School Board strictly comply with the provisions of Joint Circular
No. 1, series of 2017 of the Department of Education, Department of Budget and
Management and Department of the Interior and Local Government in order to
ensure that Special Education Fund (SEF) will be utilized for the intended purpose
or according to the identified priority areas listed in the Annual Implementation
Plan (AIP) culled from the DepEd-approved School Improvement Plan (SIP);
b. That the Local Chief Executive, as the Head of the Local School Board, adhere
strictly with the existing laws, rules and regulations in the prioritization and
utilization of Special Education Fund particularly for the operation and
maintenance of public schools, construction and repair of school buildings,
facilities and equipment, educational research, purchase of books and periodicals
and sports development as provided in Sections 100(c) and 272 of the Local
Government Code of 1991 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No.
1, s. 2017 in order to ensure an effective educational development within the
locality;
c. Require the concerned Department of Education officials to immediately submit the
above mentioned supporting documents to substantiate the transaction; and
d. Municipal Accountant ensure that the supporting documents were complete
before the processing of claims pursuant to Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree
No. 1445 to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that the Municipality duly consider the instructive guidelines on the
creation or establishment of the local economic enterprise provided in the Manual on
Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises for Local Government
Units 2016 developed and issued by the Department of Budget and Management. The
local economic enterprise shall be created or established through an ordinance
instead of a resolution. It also particularly emphasizes the effective local economic
enterprise or the enterprise that contributes to the quality service delivery goals of the
LGU. As such, it must be designed, set up, operated, and managed in accordance
with a well-prepared feasibility study and a detailed business plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned department heads
to immediately initiate action to enforce the settlement of the disallowances in
accordance with the COA Revised Rules of Procedure on the Settlement of Accounts
(RRSA). Secure documents such as the Notice of Finality of Decision/COA order of
Execution to trace back those disallowances reflected in the Receivables –
Disallowances/Charges account. Once the persons liable and their accountability
have been established issue demand letter to notify them of their liability and to
enforce collection on them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant in
coordination with Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to regularly
reconcile the balances of the accounting records with the records of the GSIS in
order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the amount Due to GSIS in the
financial statements and to do not prejudice GSIS members – employees.

We recommended that the Municipality through her Sanggunian Bayan enact
ordinance establishing the scholarship program of the Municipality pursuant to
Section 447(a)(5)(xi) of R.A. 7160 or the “Local Government Code of 1991” to provide
the specific legal basis for the program and its implementation by the local
government unit.
We further recommended that department handling the scholarship program abide by
the provisions of the implementing guidelines for its proper implementation; for
program objectives evaluation and for its accurate monitoring.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly implement the Safe Closure and
Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) with the detailed plan and corresponding cost estimate
that was duly acknowledged by the Environment and Management Bureau (EMB),
CALABARZON Region to insure that the open dumpsite closure and rehabilitation are
done in a proper manner, with environmental protection measures and safeguards in
place. We further recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board
perform its mandatory activities provided in Republic Act No. 9003 or the “Ecological
Solid Waste Management Act of 2000”.

We recommended that the LGU organize the Municipal Council for the Protection of
Children (MCPC) and activate, strengthen, and sustain it pursuant to Section 15 of
Republic Act No. 9344 or the “Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the department head/s or personnel
concerned to comply with the completion and submission to the Audit Team of the
Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for part III of CY 2018 AAR
and on every AAR thereafter.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the provisions of Sections 321
and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Article 417 454(b)of the Act Implementing
Rules and Regulations and promote the Code objectives of discouraging careless
preparation of the annual budget or any practice that undermines the integration of
planning and budgeting at the local level.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant fully comply with the
aforementioned law, rules and regulations of government accounting and auditing on
the submission of the year-end financial statements particularly of the 42 barangays
of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant to require the total disallowance in the amount of ₱1,271,804.84 to be recompensed by the former Mayor Rosario M. Apacible and that other necessary steps be taken to recover the disallowed amounts.
observations.managementAction: No action yet. The former administration has filed appeal with the Civil Service Commission.
observations.reason: No reasons were provided in the report for non-implementation. Appeal filed with the Commission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman, former MENRO, incumbent MENRO, Municipal Accountant, and other municipal officials to immediately submit lacking documents to the Audit Team for further evaluation. Non-submission would be tantamount to issuance of Notice of Suspension and eventually Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman and other concerned officials were requested to submit the lacking documents. Non-submission would result to issuance of Notice of Suspension and Notice of Disallowance.
observations.reason: Notice of Suspension has been issued by the Audit Team (NS No. 17-001-100(15-16) dated December 20, 2017).

observations.recommendation: We recommended BAC and concerned municipal officials submit explanations and record project in books for fair presentation; submit lacking documents to Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Request to submit lacking documents; Notice of Suspension and Notice of Disallowance issued by Audit Team where applicable.
observations.reason: Notice of Suspension issued by Audit Team (NS No. 17-001-100(15-16) dated December 20, 2017).

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Officials refrain from giving insurance coverage without SB resolution and refund amount where authorization lacking.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality filed Motion for Review with the Commission. (ND issued by Audit Team referenced).
observations.reason: ND No. 17-001-100(15) dated December 20, 2017 issued by the Audit Team; appeal with Commission filed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor ensure no contracts are paid without appropriation and submit supporting documents and clearances; otherwise refund the amount.
observations.managementAction: The former administration filed Motion for Review with the Commission.
observations.reason: Under review/appeal; no sufficient supporting clearances provided.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor create HR group to undertake thorough study and evaluation on actual workloads and manpower requirements to avoid unnecessary jobs and minimize government spending.
observations.managementAction: No concrete implementation noted; some steps initiated to review manpower requirements.
observations.reason: No full study done; implementation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended submission of supporting documents to prove veracity of claims and refund where appropriation lacking.
observations.managementAction: The former administration filed Motion for Review with the Commission.
observations.reason: Appeal filed; documentation under review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended refund of amounts in excess and ensure compliance with DBM Budget Circular.
observations.managementAction: Former administration filed appeal with COA; process ongoing.
observations.reason: Appeal with COA Regional Office filed; resolution pending.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that:
a) The Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee that will conduct
complete inventory taking of all the Municipal properties and prepare the
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE)
and the Report of Unserviceable Property for submission to the Municipal
Accountant and Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of every year;
b) The General Services Officer submit to the Municipal Accountant the set
of papers pertaining to the disposal of unserviceable properties for
eventual preparation of journal entry voucher as basis of deduction from
the PPE account;
c) The General Services Officer in coordination with the created Inventory
Committee reconcile Inventory Report and Property records with that of
the Accounting records. Exert extra efforts to identify/account the
properties acquired prior to 2002 in lump sum amount based on available
records of both offices. Require person responsible/accountable to request
for relief from accountability to the Commission Proper for those
properties that are missing and could not be accounted for; and
d) The Municipal Accountant and the GSO/Property Officer maintain
complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards,
respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records.

We reiterated our last year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer exert
more effort to reconcile the balances of RPT and SET Receivables.
Prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the necessary adjusting
entries to reflect the accurate balances of the accounts as of the
accounting period;
b) Direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Administrator, and Municipal
Assessor to coordinate and jointly exert extra efforts in the collection of
delinquent accounts and adopt necessary measures to enable the
maximum collection of unpaid taxes;
c) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Certified List of all Real
Property Tax Delinquencies to the Sangguniang Bayan on or before the
31st of December of the year immediately succeeding the year in which
the delinquencies were incurred, with the request for assistance in the
enforcement of the remedies for collection therein. Also, coordinate with
the Office of the Provincial Treasurer for more issuance of Warrant of
Levy;
d) Ensure that the Sangguniang Bayan enact ordinance that will enhance
collection scheme, encourage taxpayers to pay their taxes and impose
additional penalties or incentives to promote sound collection system as
provided on existing provisions of the Local Government Code; and
e) Maximize availment of all legal and administrative remedies either by
administrative action through levy on real property or judicial action.

We recommended that the Municipality headed by the Municipal Mayor
with the help of the Sangguniang Bayan revisit, revise or amend the Ordinance No.
50 as well as its Implementing Rules and Regulations for smooth and strict
implementation of the Tourism Ecological Fee Ordinance. Likewise, adopt
strategy and a clear cut policy, in the collection, implementation and monitoring
of the same.

Moreso, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to adjust the booking of collections of the said Tourism Ecological
Fee to effect the correct balances of the account and to create special account
for proper recording and monitoring of the receipts and disbursements of the
Tourism Ecological Fee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG):
a) to scrutinize the sufficiency of the winning bidder’s manpower and
equipment on all the projects to be awarded and not on an individual
project only in cases where the latter is the lowest bidder in several
projects which have similar or overlapping implementation period. This is
to oblige the winning contractor to supplement its manpower and/or
equipment to be utilized in the projects awarded to it or to disqualify it
from obtaining several awards or contracts in conformity with the
provisions of Section 34.1 and 34.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184;
b) to monitor contractors with on-going projects with other government
agencies and to coordinate with them involved in implementing
infrastructure projects not disclosed in the Statement of On-going and
Completed projects to avoid overlapping and delay in project
accomplishment and/or substandard quality of work;
c) to conduct the required processes and procedures in all the stages of the
bidding in the procurement of infrastructure and comply strictly with all
the documentary requirements as mandated by RA 9184. Non-compliance
of the required documents by the prospective bidder shall be a cause for
automatic disqualification in the eligibility and award of contract;

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Engineer to strictly and closely monitor all projects simultaneously
implemented by the contractors and prepare an individual status report per
project to be submitted to the Honorable Mayor for monitoring purposes and in
order to check, verify and evaluate the quality of work and efficiency of the said
contractors.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Engineer and his Inspectorate Team to:
· conduct strict compliance to detailed engineering activities; conduct
close monitoring of projects to avoid delays on its implementation;
· submit copy of Statement of Work Accomplished for unperformed
portion as of the target completion date signed by the Municipal
Engineer and duly acknowledged by the contractor on the number of
days delayed;
· continuously impose strict deduction of Liquidated Damages on
delays not feasibly avoided; and
b) MPDC/BAC Chairman to ensure that the programs/projects/activities are
properly planned before undergoing bidding process to avoid problems
during the implementation stage that may cause delay and to assist the
Municipal Engineer on project monitoring.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Direct the MENRO and Municipal Engineer to implement the Safe
Closure and Rehabilitation Plan in accordance with the Memorandum of
Agreement with the Department of Environment and Natural Resources-
Environmental Management Bureau (DENR-EMB);
2. Strictly enforce the Ordinansa Blg. 79 Taon 2017 entitled “Ordinasang
Nagtatakda ng Pagbabawal sa Paggamit ng Styrofoam at Plastic Bags
sa mga Dry Goods at Pagsasaayos ng mga Plastic Materials sa Paggamit
nito sa mga Wet Goods sa Munisipalidad ng Nasugbu, Batangas at mga
Itinakdang Karampatang Parusa” and “No Segregation, No Collection
Policy”; and enjoin the barangay officials for effective implementation;
3. Direct the MENRO to see to it that the equipment/mechanisms installed
in the Materials Recovery Facility in the Dumpsite will be utilized in
accordance to its purpose and to coordinate with the Municipal
Agriculturist for the revival of the operation of vermin-composting;
4. Direct the MENRO to exert more efforts for the approval of the 10-year
Solid Waste Management Plan and its implementation;
5. Take appropriate action for establishment of the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board; and
Further, the Sangguniang Bayan to pass an adopting ordinance for the
approval and strict implementation of the 10-Year Solid Waste Management
Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Revisit and update Executive Order No. 2017-004and furnish copy to the
members of Municipal Project Monitoring Committee (MPMC), reminding
them of the duties and responsibilities assigned to them for efficient and
effective implementation of the projects; Re-organize and include members
with technical knowledge, experience or expertise on projects/activities; and
b) Require the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator and the MPMC to monitor strictly
and supervise ADM projects and other nationally and locally public works
projects of the LGU, to achieve maximum quality of the finished projects to
the benefit of the government and the general public.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor seek authority from the
Sangguniang Bayan (SB) for the reversion of PP4,296,986.79 and P127,927.85,
to Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund and Special Education Fund,
respectively. Require the Municipal Accountant to revisit the file of
Disbursement Vouchers as listed in the schedule and the corresponding
subsidiary ledgers of the claimants to finalize reversion and subsequently to
prepare the necessary journal entry to reflect the correct Accounts Payable
balances as of the end of year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Honorable
Members of the legislative and the concerned officials work together and support
the strict implementation of the revised Revenue Code of 2014.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Market
Supervisor to 1) act upon the various causes of losses. 2) ensure prudence in the
disbursement of personal services and 3) strictly impose collection of market
delinquency.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Administrator, MPDC and Municipal Engineer to coordinate and work closely to
ensure full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the efficient and
effective attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget
Officer to allocate fund for the MOOE of Municipal Disaster Risk and Reduction
Management Office. Refrain from charging against theLDRRMF non-related
expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the members of the
Local School Board to utilize fully the Special Education Fund for the benefit of the
students and formulate a three-year SEF program considering the
programs/projects enumerated in Section 272 of Republic Act 7160 as implemented
by DedEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circulars No. 01 s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017.
Refrain from charging non-priority expenses from the fund and immediately submit
the necessary documents for the disbursement amounting to ₱113,000.00 for further
evaluation and audit.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the GAA provision
that monthly take home pay of employee shall not be lower than Four Thousand
Pesos (₱4,000.00).

We recommended that the Municipality (a) reconcile employees’ records at
GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify the affected employees on the
unremitted balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies; (b)
identify the cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper
adjustments and (c) remit the due GSIS loan amortizations pursuant to Section
14.2, Rule III of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Government Service
Insurance Act of 1997 or R.A. No. 8291 to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary
financial expenses in the form of imposed penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the municipal records with the Provincial Accountant and
settle discrepancies between the balances of reciprocal accounts so that the
Municipality will be able to present the correct balances of inter-agency
receivables and payables in the financial statements.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant request from the Provincial Accountant
the copies of the JEVs showing all the transactions recorded in the books of
accounts of the Province that should be taken up in the Municipality’s books of
accounts.

We recommended that the Management evaluate strictly effect the necessary
processes to expedite the implementation of the not- yet started and on-going
projects for the timely and immediate benefits of the municipality’s constituents.
Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to evaluate the outstanding Due to Other NGAs accounts amounting to ₱509,212.48
and effect appropriate disposition/return to the source agency or to the National
Treasury pursuant to Section 122 of GAAM, Volume I and Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of
COA Circular No. 94-013. Likewise, continuously monitor the implementation of
all on-going projects to maximize its purpose for the benefit of its constituents.

observations.managementAction: “The Local Chief Executive of the Municipality already issued Executive Order creating Inventory Committee that designated Municipal GSO, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer as the key officers to work out for the reconciliation of the accounts. The key officers will convene on August to plan for the reconciliation of the accounts.”
observations.reason: The task to reconcile and conduct physical inventory will commence on August 2018.

observations.managementAction:
“The Municipal Accountant used the assessment roll provided by the Municipal Assessor as basis for set-up of RPT and SET receivables, while the Municipal Treasurer submitted the Report of Delinquent account showing a total summary of Php 1,050,474.00 as of December 2017, in this reports includes penalties and surcharges, properties under court litigation, CARP and CLOA, properties in the name of Municipal Government and others instead of Certified List of Taxpayer and the amount due discrepancy of Php 667,824,667.20 from the reported balances of RPT and SET receivables amounting to Php 382,650,164.28 set by the Municipal Accountant.

In this regard, the Municipal Treasurer is doing extra effort to prepare a Certified List of Taxpayer with the amount due and collectible for the year then furnish and reconcile it to the Municipal Accountant for proper booking-up of receivables.”
observations.reason: The manpower assigned to do the task was not sufficient.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the amount recorded under Accounts Payable and corresponding asset/expense account by ₱6,829,287.07 to present the correct account balances at year–end.
observations.managementAction: The documents provided and necessary adjustments were drawn as JEV.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a) The Municipal Accountant to record the Land in Barangay XI purchased in the amount of ₱619,565.00 and adjust the Land account by the appraised value of all the donated real properties. Reconcile the Accounting with related records of the other departments for reconciliation of values in the books of accounts.
b) The Municipal Budget Officer to allocate in the ensuing years’ budget the cost for titling of Land; and
c) The Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and MPDC to coordinate with each other and exert extra efforts to secure the documents relative to the purchase made from Mr. Reynaldo Cargado. Work together towards the reconciliation and registration of all landholdings to protect the municipality against undue risk of third party claim.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Code is not applicable here. (Note: management comments shown in table: “The GSO already maintain related records; the appraised values are subject to reconciliation.”)
observations.reason: For 2018, budget will be set up to be used for titling of properties under the name of the Municipality. After titling of all properties, adjustments will be done by the Municipal Accountant in cooperation of other departments for reconciliation of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer to charge expenses from Engineering, Supervision and Administrative Overhead related only to the projects to be implemented. The Municipal Engineer no longer include ESAO in the preparation of Program of Work.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already stop the practice of drawing journal entry voucher to correct the balances of the accounts affected for fair presentation of financial statements. The Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging ESAO to the bidded projects. Journal Entry Voucher No. 18-05-07 dated May 31, 2018 was drawn as of report writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the municipal officials concerned to undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual budget on the basis of reasonable and/or realistic projection of revenues for the ensuing year, in accordance with the intent of local budgeting that will support the budget for each year. Moreover, the Business and Permit Licensing Office, exert extra effort in the issuance of business permit for the accounted difference of 232 and continuous campaign, inspection and monitoring of new businesses subject to issuance of permits. Likewise, the operation of market and slaughterhouse and cemetery be given preferred attention.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Administrator has taken measures to maximize the earning of the LGU. The Market Administrator is now more careful in projecting the forecast as basis for the budget. Still the finance committee is careful in projecting the forecast as its basis for the budget.
observations.reason: The manpower and time constraints; continuous campaign and collection efforts are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Development Council to ensure full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the efficient and effective attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes. Also revisit and ensure public notification of PPA features/elements per COA Circular.
observations.managementAction: The unutilized balance was already used to fund projects in CY 2017. Projects under the 20% Development Fund were clearly identified in the AIP and Annual Budget. Public notification is now being observed upon validation by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Delay in release of IRA affected implementation timing; actions on public notification and PPMP, PAP and APP still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to: a) exert extra effort to properly coordinate with each other in order to achieve efficiently and effectively the goals of the fund; b) expedite the procurement process to serve the Day Care children within the school year covered by the Supplementary Feeding Program; and c) to maintain logbooks and submit documents for full liquidation and turnover of documents from one department to another for full liquidation.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO completed the implementation of SFP for SY 2017-2018 using the fund amounting to ₱3,978,000.00 from DSWD FO IV-A. Liquidation was submitted to DSWD FO IV-A on February 21, 2018 and documents are being processed (as of March 15, 2018).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Honorable Mayor and all department heads prepare and submit their Annual PPMP, prepare PAP and APP, firm up PPMP, PAP and APP of all departments; and direct the concerned officials to stop procuring infrastructure without approved PPMP and APP.
observations.managementAction: The PPMP has been accomplished and submitted. All departments were given notice to accomplish the PPMP, PAP and APP of all projects. An Inspectorate Team was created to implement and closely monitor projects.
observations.reason: Some projects still not included; PPMP, PAP and APP preparation continuing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) adopt and enforce the GAD Code; b) require the MPDC to spearhead setting up and maintenance of GAD database; c) create LGU GFPS or TWG who will provide technical assistance ensuring the GAD Database is operational; d) remind the Sangguniang Bayan to take necessary actions to pass a GAD Code; and e) ensure utilization of 5% of annual budget for GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan is being implemented; accomplishment reports were submitted to Provincial Office for compliance; GAD Code prepared and forwarded to Sanggunian Bayan for approval.
observations.reason: Some recommendations ongoing; GAD database not fully established; GAD Code pending approval (now submitted).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the outstanding Due to Other NGAs accounts amounting to ₱1,004,975.91 and effect appropriate disposition/return to the source agency or to the National Treasury pursuant to Section 122 of GAAM, Volume I and Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013. Likewise, continuously monitor the implementation of all on-going projects to maximize its purpose for the benefit of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office will follow up the concerned LGU counterpart of the agencies to whom the refund will be made for the unutilized balance of grant of respective national government agencies. Once the concerned department has pinpointed the payee, then checks will be drawn to such payee.
observations.reason: Some amounts already remitted; others remain to be reconciled with concerned agencies; follow-ups ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher to take up the unrecorded properties and the completed infrastructure projects in the books and its corresponding depreciation to reflect the Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment and Government Equity.
observations.managementAction: The Journal Entry Voucher No. 18-05-08 dated May 31, 2018 has been drawn as of report writing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other Local School Board members refrain from charging non-priority expenses against SEF and adhere to authorized expenditures; require refund of the amount of P721,228.73 and require officials to submit documents/proof of disbursement; and refrain from charging non-priority expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Effective July 2018, any request for reimbursement of communication allowance charged to SEF will no longer be processed. DepEd District Supervisors were requested to furnish 5-10 year development plan for coordination.
observations.reason: Some reimbursements and non-priority charges were corrected; supporting documents for certain disbursements are being submitted for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct concerned offices to: a) undertake cost-benefit analysis; b) coordinate with barangays for strict segregation; c) devise plan/strategy among barangay officials to maximize use of containers; and d) provide justification why procurement was made despite previous audit observations and furnish lacking documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is committed to make sure that the containers are being utilized; MENRO to coordinate with barangays; studies to be conducted. Some documents submitted to COA TAS.
observations.reason: Some facilities not operational; some procurements not properly used; justification being studied and documents being furnished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 1) Direct the MENRO and Municipal Engineer to implement the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan in accordance with the MOA with DENR-EMB; 2) Strictly enforce Ordinansa Blg. 79 and 'No Segregation, No Collection Policy' and enjoin barangay officials; 3) Direct the MENRO to ensure equipment/mechanisms installed in the MRF are utilized as intended and coordinate with the Municipal Agriculturist for vermi-composting revival; 4) Direct the MENRO to exert more efforts for approval of the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and its implementation; 5) Take appropriate action for establishment of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; and Sangguniang Bayan to pass adopting ordinance.
observations.managementAction: DENR provided financial assistance. The Municipal Engineer to coordinate on the alternative waste management after completion of safe closure. Ordinance No. 79 S. 2017 passed. Equipment utilization to be improved. Inspectorate and action plans underway.
observations.reason: Closure and rehabilitation ongoing; NSWMC approval pending; MRF equipment not fully utilized; Municipal Solid Waste Management Board not yet established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWDO and Municipal Agriculturist to ensure the effectiveness, efficiency and success of the projects by proper planning and monitoring, regular reporting schedule to update on implementation; and to submit explanation or justification for the utilization of fund for meat processing project amounting to ₱160,000.00.
observations.managementAction: MSWDO explained root of the problem and acceptance of responsibility; projects from NGAs implemented; MSWDO to focus and follow guidelines in every program. Letters and replies were provided to COA.
observations.reason: Some projects ongoing; some documents and justifications submitted; some issues require further validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 1) exercise diligence in procurement planning and execution; 2) conduct consultation, study and evaluation considering feasibility and effectiveness; 3) require MENRO Head to submit justification why facilities procured were not properly utilized and maintained by the recipients; 4) submit justification why another procurement was made despite previous audit observations; and 5) furnish COA TAS the lacking documents required.
observations.managementAction: MENRO to coordinate with barangays; required documents being studied. Some documents submitted to COA TAS. MENRO to monitor and prepare reports.
observations.reason: Some facilities not utilized optimally; justification and documentation being prepared; follow-up ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipality to: 1) exert more efforts for approval of 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan; 2) take action for establishment of Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; 3) strictly implement No Segregation, No Collection Policy; 4) provide justification for incurrence of rental; 5) expedite safe closure and rehabilitation; and 6) allocate budget for purchase of land for dumpsite and landfill compliance with RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Engineer to coordinate with Office of the Mayor regarding alternative waste management; rental expenses included in rehabilitation plan; Municipality already owned bulldozer but rented for operations; plan to purchase land considered.
observations.reason: Rehabilitation ongoing; rentals incurred to avoid disruption; Board not yet established; plan pending NSWMC approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure appropriated funds be utilized solely for the specific purpose and refund amount of ₱900,000.00 disbursed without corresponding appropriation for capital outlay; and ensure that appropriated funds are used for capital outlay if intended.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant drew a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the amount charged to MOOE to appropriate fund account. (ND issued by Audit Team referenced).
observations.reason: Implementation actions taken; ND issued by Audit Team. Repair of dump truck and other matters addressed in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU ensure that all disbursements are properly authorized by Sanggunian Bayan Resolutions/Ordinances and that procurement planning be based on approved AIP and budget ceilings.
observations.managementAction: Recommendations noted and actions taken; Sanggunian resolutions obtained for subsequent utilization; budget realignments observed and corrective measures implemented.
observations.reason: Some prior realignments occurred without SB approval but were rectified; further compliance enforced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor coordinate and secure copy of the approved guidelines for grant of PBB from DILG and submit the same to the Audit Team. Otherwise, refund the amount paid.
observations.managementAction: The former administration filed appeal with the Commission. The LGU to secure DILG guidelines and submit to COA.
observations.reason: Appeal with Commission; documentation being processed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal officials strictly adhere to Section 325(a) of RA 7160 on PS cap and refrain from incurring additional Personal Services and require concerned officials and employees to refund disallowed amounts.
observations.managementAction: Documents and appeals filed; some positions reviewed. Management working on compliance.
observations.reason: Appeal and compliance actions ongoing; some positions adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer require the contractor to submit the lacking documents so that a complete technical review and evaluation of the contracts and reported work accomplishments be conducted by COA TAS.
observations.managementAction: Documents were submitted and technical reviews scheduled; letters sent to contractors for compliance.
observations.reason: Some lacking documents submitted; COA TAS to complete evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure mandatory obligations such as electric utilities be considered in preparation and approval of Annual Budget and to refrain from payments without appropriation and require refund where appropriate.
observations.managementAction: Utilities obligations for ensuing year included and accounted as payables at year end. No refund required as management corrected recording.
observations.reason: Now included in payables at year-end; no refund made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended cost-benefit and coordination with barangays, design plans to maximize utility, mini-truck allocation for garbage collection etc.
observations.managementAction: Coordination with barangays ongoing; MENRO to monitor utilization and train barangay officials.
observations.reason: Issues remain with utilization; monitoring and coordination ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended submission of authority to travel from DILG Regional Office and other supporting documentation; otherwise refund the amounts.
observations.managementAction: Justifications being processed and documentation requested; some travel authority obtained resulting in partial compliance.
observations.reason: Some justifications submitted; further documentation required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended require contractor to conduct corrective measures; submit core bore tests; submit lacking documentary requirements; exercise prudence in preparation of work program; contractor to refund excessive contract costs where applicable.
observations.managementAction: Corrective measures implemented; core bore tests submitted; contractors required to make corrections; enforcement of refunds under process.
observations.reason: Corrective works and documentation submitted; enforcement of refunds ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended heads and personnel allocate funds for yearly registration and insurance and recover costs where applicable; ensure transfer of vehicle titles, and secure insurance.
observations.managementAction: Some vehicles now registered and insured; some insurance coverage pending due to budget constraints.
observations.reason: Some vehicles complied; some remain without insurance due to lack of budget; transfers being processed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended refund of amounts in excess and ensure compliance with DBM circular; require refund of excess PEI.
observations.managementAction: Former administration filed appeal with COA Regional Office; appeal submitted.
observations.reason: Appeal with COA Regional Office; further actions pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant ensure completeness of documentary requirements before processing payments and avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured completeness of documents before processing payments as corrective measure.
observations.reason: Some vehicles regularized; others pending transfer and documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended negotiate for increase of credit line limit; stop practice of paying gasoline on reimbursement basis and ensure payments be made check payable to supplier pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002; comply with BIR withholding on payments.
observations.managementAction: LGU already has a credit line with two gasoline stations; Municipal Treasurer stop reimbursements and ensure PO-based purchases as required.
observations.reason: Gasoline purchase procedures aligned with COA circular and POs now used.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61; stop issuance of gasoline on weekends except 24/7 operations; ensure drivers' trip tickets with required serial numbering; submit Monthly Report of Official Travels, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Man-Hours for heavy equipment.
observations.managementAction: Drivers’ Trip Tickets are now attached with required information and serial numbers; monthly reports and monitoring instituted.
observations.reason: Some controls improved; full monthly reporting and man-hours submission ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor: a) refrain from charging non-priority items to LDRRMF; b) refund/revert the amount of ₱1,097,550.00 back to LDRRMF; c) ensure utilization of fund for disaster preparedness.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is now careful in utilization of LDRRM Fund; MDRRMO to oversee implementation and utilize vehicles and FB van as rescue vehicles; some equipment returned to MDRRMO; Inspectorate Team monitoring.
observations.reason: Some items returned; refund/reversion process in progress; control measures instituted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that concerned officials settle/liquidate advances; the Municipal Accountant monitor regularly and withhold salaries where applicable.
observations.managementAction: Liquidations were implemented; only one cash advance remained unsettled as noted; Municipal Accountant observed the recommendation.
observations.reason: One remaining unsettled advance (Rufino Hernandez ₱6,500.00) remained due to absence of reimbursement source; collection attempts ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that BAC and TWG ensure honoraria payments supported with complete documentation and, where overpaid, refunds to be made.
observations.managementAction: BAC/TWG to submit supporting documents and refunds required where excess found.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents submitted; some refunds being processed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended refund of overpayment and strict adherence to Local Budget Circular No. 103 provisions regarding transportation allowances.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant complied with the guidelines; concerned department heads to refund overpayment of ₱101,250.00.
observations.reason: Reconciliations ongoing; refunds to be effected where required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended refund of excess PEI and adherence to DBM circular.
observations.managementAction: Appeal filed with the Commission; implementation pending.
observations.reason: Appeal filed; some actions pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor require Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to request COA for write-off of dormant accounts and provide certification and aging schedule and reasons why records could not be located.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Administrator stated he will initiate request to COA to write off dormant accounts; SB resolution requested for write-off.
observations.reason: Sangguniang Bayan resolution requested; process ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor create council/committee to design and report on program activities and identify affected families and confirm beneficiaries from the receipt of P1.2M from Fuego Land Corporation.
observations.managementAction: Housing program implemented; affected families from Barangay Natipuan are living in specified housing; titling and documentation ongoing.
observations.reason: Program implemented partially; titling and documentation pending verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor require Municipal Accountant to record audit disallowances as Receivables–Disallowances/Charges and provide subsidiary records and disclosures; secure documentation and follow up settlements.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant to initiate COA write-off requests; efforts to reconcile and secure records ongoing.
observations.reason: Sangguniang Bayan requested write-off; COA process to be followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct NDGH inventories be validated by physical count and reconciled and recorded as PPE or Other Assets, evaluated for appraised/fair market value and recorded accordingly.
observations.managementAction: NDGH inventories to be inventoried and reconciled; some inventories submitted.
observations.reason: NDGH inventory not yet submitted to COA; reconciliation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: See specific recommendations for each prior observation including refunds, documentation, implementation of controls, and submission of required documents to COA.
observations.managementAction: Several actions taken including submission of documentation, appeals filed with COA/CSC, establishment of procedures, and corrective JE Vs.
observations.reason: Some issues resolved; others remain pending appeals or further documentary support and corrective actions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the amount of recorded under Accounts Payable and the corresponding asset or expense account by ₱6,829,287.07  to present the correct account balances at  year–end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Municipal Accountant to record the Land in Barangay XI  purchased in the amount of  ₱619,565.00 and adjust the Land  account by the appraised value of all the donated real properties.  Reconcile Accounting with related records of the landholdings of the municipality;
The Municipal Budget Officer to allocate in  the ensuing  years’ budget the cost for titling of Land; and
The Municipal Assessor, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant,  Municipal Budget Officer and MPDC to coordinate  with each other  and exert extra efforts to secure the documents relative to the purchase made from Mr. Reynaldo Cargado. Work    together   towards the reconciliation and registration of all landholdings to protect the municipality  against undue risk of third party claim.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry voucher to correct the balances of the accounts affected for fair presentation of  financial statements, and the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligation of  ESAO in addition to the project costs.
Likewise, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer to charge expenses from Engineering, Supervision and Administrative Overhead related only to the projects to be implemented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the municipal officials concerned to undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual budget on the basis of reasonable and/or realistic projection of revenues for the ensuing year, in accordance with the intent of local government budgeting that will support the proper allocation of expenditures as provided under Section 316 of RA 7160.  Moreover, the Business and Permit Licensing Office, exert extra effort in the issuance of business permit for  the accounted difference of 232 and continuous campaign, inspection and monitoring of new businesses subject to issuance of permits. Likewise,  the operation of market and slaughterhouse and  cemetery be given preferred attention.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local Development Council to ensure  full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the efficient and effective attainment of desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor again instructs  the Local Development Council to identify priority developmental programs and projects chargeable against the 20% Development Fund as provided in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and the DBM.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal Engineer to publicize the projects/programs/activities in conspicuous places within the agency premises and in the project venue or notify the public on its essential features/elements in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials and employees:
To exert extra effort to properly coordinate with each other in order to achieve efficiently  and effectively  the  goals of the Supplementary Feeding Program;
To expedite the procurement process to serve the Day Care children within the school year covered by the program; and
To maintain logbooks for the incoming/outgoing  and turn-over of documents from one department to immediately identify the cause of delays in the transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require all department heads to prepare and submit their respective PPMP for their programs, activities and projects (PAPs) for eventual consolidation into  APP. Firm up the PPMP based on the approved AIP, taking into consideration the available resources, particularly the  budget ceilings; and
Direct the concerned officials to stop the practice of procuring infrastructure without the approved Project Procurement Management Plan and  Annual Procurement Plan.  Otherwise, any procurement not included in the approved PPMP and APP  shall not be allowed in audit.

We recommended that the incumbent Municipal Mayor require to recompense the total disallowance  in the amount of   ₱1,271,804.84  through  collection of  the same from the former Mayor as the salary and benefits paid from the time they were  appointed shall be the personal liability of former Mayor Rosario M. Apacible, pursuant to CSC Decision 170088.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Accountant to evaluate the outstanding Due to Other NGAs accounts amounting to ₱1,004,975.91 and effect appropriate disposition/return to the source agency or to the National Treasury pursuant  to Section 122 of GAAM, Volume I and Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up  the unrecorded properties and the completed infrastructure projects in the books and its corresponding depreciation to reflect  fairly  the Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment  and Government Equity.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor and other Local School Board to refrain  from charging non-priority expenditures against the Special Education Fund and adhere to the provisions of DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 with supplemental provisions under Joint Circular No. 01-A and 01-B dated March 14, 2000 and June 25, 2001, respectively, to ensure that the purpose for which the fund was established is met.  Refund the amount of   P721,228.73; and
Ms. Avelina O. Gaa and Ms. Shirley G. David,  submit official receipts/other proof of disbursement of their cash advances to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit and to refund the unutilized cash advances.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Mayor and other Local School Board to refrain  from charging non-priority expenditures against the Special Education Fund and adhere to the provisions of DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 with supplemental provisions under Joint Circular No. 01-A and 01-B dated March 14, 2000 and June 25, 2001, respectively, to ensure that the purpose for which the fund was established is met.  Refund the amount of   P721,228.73; and
Ms. Avelina O. Gaa and Ms. Shirley G. David,  submit official receipts/other proof of disbursement of their cash advances to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit and to refund the unutilized cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the concerned LGU Officials observe strictly the guidelines of the NDRRMC DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular with focused on the proper utilization of the LDRRMF;
Instruct the OIC-MDRRMO oversee the implementation of LDRRMF projects for disaster preparedness and mitigation. Adopt a sound and clear  LDRRM Plan for the Municipality; and
Instruct the Municipal Administrator to:  a) require the Head of Offices concerned using the motorcycles to allocate budget and return  the amount of each motorcycles to LDRRMF; and  b)  turn over the FB van as rescue vehicles to the Local Risk Reduction Management Office.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the provisions of Article 210 to 213 and 215 of RA 7160 and Section 3.4 of National Budget Circular No. 563 April 22, 2016.  Contributions and elective officials expenses related to leagues which are not included in the preceding articles are not government expenses and should not be charged against the funds of the municipality.     In addition, the previously mentioned budget circular specifically disallows individual membership fees paid to private organizations. Hence, refund the total amount of ₱50,500.00.

We recommended that the  Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman, former MENRO, incumbent MENRO, Mr. Scott Countryman,  Municipal Accountant,  and other municipal officials concerned to   coordinate and immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team for further evaluation. Non-submission would tantamount to issuance of  Notice of Suspension and eventually Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the MSWDO and Municipal Agriculturist  to ensure the effectiveness, efficiency and success of the projects by proper planning, sound monitoring system, supervision  and regular reporting period to update the implementation of a project in order to help the beneficiaries attain satisfactorily the   benefits therefrom.
Submit explanation or justification for the utilization of the fund for meat processing project amounting to ₱160,000.00 without any concrete results as to its implementation.
